message:
I want to build a gamified habit-tracking web app. The architecture will be custom HTML/CSS/vanilla JavaScript on the frontend, preparing for a Supabase backend later.

Design Rules: I want a minimal, sleek interface inspired by 'Variant-style' design—dark mode, subtle 1px borders, CSS variables for theming, no generic Tailwind components. It must be mobile-first.

Task 1: The Shell. > Create the index.html, style.css, and app.js files.
Set up a layout with a fixed bottom navigation containing exactly three tabs: SPIN, Wallet, and Me.
Make the main content area dynamic so clicking a tab switches the view.
In style.css, set up CSS variables for the background, text, borders, and exactly six distinct, vibrant 'token' colors (e.g., neon green, cyan, magenta, etc.). Let's build this foundational UI first, ensuring it feels like a polished consumer product.

Task 2: State Management & Random Token Logic.
Now that the UI shell is built, let's implement the core JavaScript logic in app.js.

Requirements:

    Set up a state object to track the user's wallet (an inventory counting the balance of all 6 token colors) and total lifetime tokens earned.

    Create a completeHabit() function. When called, it should randomly select one of the 6 token colors (equal 1/6 probability) and add it to the wallet.

    Create a function to check for "Triplets". It should scan the wallet and return a boolean if the user has 3 or more of any single color.

    Wire this up to the UI so that the Wallet view dynamically updates to show the current counts for each color. If a color has 3 or more tokens, give its CSS grid item a subtle glowing border to indicate a usable triplet.

Task 3: Spin Mechanics and Fixed Rewards.
Now we need to implement the slot machine logic in app.js and update the SPIN view.

Requirements:

    Reward State: Define an object holding one fixed, customizable reward string for each tier (T1, T2, T3, Jackpot).

    Betting Tiers: Create three betting options:

        Level 1 (Cost: 1 token of any color): Eligible only for T1 and Miss.

        Level 2 (Cost: 3 tokens of the same color): Eligible for T1, T2, and Miss.

        Level 3 (Cost: 3 matching tokens + 1 token of any color): Eligible for all tiers (T1, T2, T3, Jackpot) and Miss.

    The Engine: Create a spin(betLevel) function. It must first deduct the correct tokens from the wallet state. Then, it calculates the outcome.

    The Odds: The global base odds are: Miss (9%), T1 (40%), T2 (30%), T3 (20%), Jackpot (1%). When a lower-level bet is placed, mathematically normalize the odds for the eligible tiers only (e.g., for Level 1, split 100% proportionally between the base weights of T1 and Miss).

    UI Update: Wire this to the SPIN tab. Add three buttons for the bet levels (disable them if the wallet lacks the required tokens). When clicked, display a simple text output of the spin result and show the unlocked reward if they win.

Task 4: The Habit Hub and Completing the Loop.
We need to build out the "Me" tab in index.html and app.js to complete the local frontend loop.

Requirements:

    Habit State: Create a simple array of habit objects in JS. Use these defaults for testing: "Prep overnight oats", "Write 500 words for DPhil proposal", "Badminton practice", and "Clear email inbox". Each should have an id, title, and isCompleted boolean.

    Sleek UI: Render this list in the "Me" tab. Do not use standard HTML checkboxes. Create a custom, minimal toggle or circular checkbox using CSS that fits our dark, Variant-style design language.

    The Trigger: When a habit is marked complete, trigger the completeHabit() function we built earlier.

    Visual Feedback: When the habit is checked off, provide immediate micro-feedback. Briefly flash the specific neon color of the random token they just earned next to the habit name, so the user knows exactly what dropped into their wallet.

    Daily Reset: Add a subtle "Reset Day" button at the bottom of the Me tab for testing purposes, which unchecks all habits so we can simulate multiple days of earning.
